cta.title: Not Sure Whether We're a Good Match for Your Needs?
cta.description: Connect with our team to schedule a no-cost consultation and share the health concerns that matter most to you.

hero.title: Conditions We Help You Navigate
hero.subtitle: Whether you're managing day-to-day discomfort or navigating a complex, long-term health concern, Traditional Chinese Medicine offers natural, evidence-informed care across a wide range of conditions.

categories.name: Pain Management
categories.subtitle: Drug-free, compassionate care for acute injuries as well as persistent, long-term pain
categories.description: Through acupuncture and TCM therapies, we support people dealing with lower back pain, upper back pain, and sciatica — whether symptoms appeared suddenly or have lingered over time — all without the need for medications. - In TCM, neck and shoulder pain is commonly attributed to Qi stagnation and blood stasis. - Acupuncture is applied to release trigger points and reduce localized tension. - Tui Na works to loosen tight muscles, while cupping promotes healthier circulation in the affected area.

categories.name: Mental & Emotional Wellbeing
categories.description: Warm, attentive support for stress, anxiety, depression, and your overall emotional health

categories.name: Digestive Health
categories.description: Focused care for digestive conditions and the long-term health of your gut

categories.name: Women's Health
categories.description: Whole-person care that thoughtfully addresses a broad range of women's health concerns

categories.name: Respiratory Issues
categories.description: Nurturing support for respiratory health and easier, healthier breathing

categories.name: Immune System
categories.description: Strengthen your resilience and encourage your body's innate ability to protect itself

conditions.title: Back Pain
conditions.description: For lower back pain, upper back pain, and sciatica — whether symptoms came on suddenly or have been present for years — acupuncture and TCM therapies provide meaningful support without relying on drugs or surgical intervention.
conditions.tcmApproach: Within TCM, back pain is viewed as an interruption to the flow of Qi and blood through the body's channels. Our practitioners use acupuncture to clear these blockages, cupping to encourage healthy circulation, and herbal medicine to help ease inflammation and strengthen the back over time.

conditions.title: Neck & Shoulder Pain
conditions.description: Whether neck and shoulder tension, stiffness, or aching traces back to stress, posture, or a past injury, targeted natural therapies can help bring relief.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM commonly attributes neck and shoulder pain to Qi stagnation and blood stasis within the involved channels. Our approach draws on acupuncture to release trigger points, Tui Na to relieve muscle tightness, and cupping to encourage better circulation through the area.

conditions.title: Arthritis & Joint Pain
symptoms: Pain that worsens with shifts in weather or temperature
conditions.description: We offer natural approaches to help manage pain related to osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and general joint inflammation.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, arthritis falls under the category of 'Bi Syndrome,' in which pathogenic factors such as wind, cold, dampness, or heat obstruct the joints. Treatment focuses on clearing those factors, calming inflammation, and building the body's overall strength and resilience.

conditions.title: Headaches & Migraines
conditions.description: We provide a medication-free approach to meaningful relief for those living with tension headaches, migraines, and recurring head pain.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM identifies several distinct headache patterns, including Liver Yang rising, Blood deficiency, and external pathogenic influences. By determining which pattern fits your experience, we build a personalized treatment plan using acupuncture and herbal medicine to address the underlying cause.

conditions.title: Anxiety & Panic Disorders
conditions.description: Natural, drug-free care for people living with anxiety, panic attacks, and ongoing feelings of nervousness.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, anxiety is often connected to Heart and Kidney disharmony, Liver Qi stagnation, or a disturbance of the Shen (spirit). Treatment aims to calm the Shen, restore the smooth movement of Qi, and nourish the relevant organ systems to bring emotional balance back into harmony.

conditions.title: Depression & Low Mood
symptoms: Difficulty with concentration and mental clarity
conditions.description: Holistic, whole-person support for individuals navigating depression, low energy, and a diminished sense of motivation.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM frequently associates depression with Liver Qi stagnation, Heart Blood deficiency, or Spleen Qi deficiency. Treatment focuses on moving stagnant Qi, addressing the root deficiencies at play, and gently fostering an improvement in mood and overall vitality.

conditions.title: Insomnia & Sleep Disorders
symptoms: Difficulty falling asleep when the night begins
conditions.description: Natural, caring support for people who have difficulty falling asleep, wake repeatedly during the night, or simply never feel rested no matter how many hours they spend in bed.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM views disrupted sleep as frequently rooted in an imbalance between the Heart and Kidney, internal heat arising from Yin deficiency, or an unsettled Shen. To help restore genuinely restorative sleep, our care focuses on nourishing Yin, calming the Heart, and grounding the Shen.

conditions.title: Stress & Burnout
conditions.description: When prolonged stress or burnout has worn down your sense of wellbeing, we offer care aimed at restoring inner balance and helping you rebuild resilience.
conditions.tcmApproach: Sustained stress gradually depletes Qi and causes Liver stagnation throughout the body. Our approach moves and regulates Liver Qi, addresses the deficiencies that have built up over time, and incorporates practical stress management strategies — supporting you in rebuilding balance and cultivating lasting resilience.

conditions.title: IBS & Digestive Issues
conditions.description: Focused care for irritable bowel syndrome, bloating, gas, and the discomfort that comes with everyday digestive difficulties.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, IBS often reflects a disharmony between the Liver and Spleen, where emotional stress directly disrupts digestive function. We address this pattern by moving Liver Qi, strengthening Spleen function, and working to bring the digestive system back into harmony.

conditions.title: Acid Reflux & GERD
conditions.description: Drawing on a natural TCM approach, we offer supportive care for heartburn, acid reflux, and gastroesophageal reflux disease.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM understands reflux as Stomach Qi traveling in the wrong direction — a pattern that is often accompanied by heat or phlegm. Treatment works to redirect Stomach Qi downward, clear associated heat, and build overall digestive strength.

conditions.title: Constipation
conditions.description: Attentive, effective care for those dealing with chronic constipation or unpredictable bowel patterns.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, constipation can stem from several different patterns — among them Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, Yin deficiency, or Spleen Qi weakness. Because the underlying cause differs from one person to the next, treatment is individually tailored with the aim of restoring healthy, regular bowel function.

conditions.title: Fertility Support
conditions.description: Comprehensive, whole-person support for both women and men — whether you are working toward natural conception or preparing for IVF.
conditions.tcmApproach: Our care focuses on regulating menstrual cycles, nourishing Kidney Essence (Jing), promoting healthy blood flow to the reproductive organs, and reducing stress. Treatment plans are shaped individually around each partner's unique patterns and needs.

conditions.title: Menstrual Disorders
conditions.description: Natural, supportive care addressing painful periods, irregular cycles, PMS, and a broad range of menstrual-related concerns.
conditions.tcmApproach: Many menstrual concerns involve Liver Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, or Cold lodged in the uterus. Our treatment works to regulate the cycle, nourish and move Blood, support the smooth flow of Qi, and clear any pathogenic factors contributing to symptoms.

conditions.title: Menopause Symptoms
conditions.description: Warm, natural support for the menopausal transition — including hot flashes, night sweats, mood changes, and other associated symptoms.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM views menopause as a natural decline in both Kidney Yin and Yang. Our care nourishes Kidney Yin, works to bring Yin and Yang into greater harmony, and calms the Shen — all with the goal of easing this transition and reducing its associated symptoms.

conditions.title: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome)
conditions.description: Using a whole-person perspective, we support hormonal balance, work toward restoring regular cycles, and help manage the symptoms associated with PCOS.
conditions.tcmApproach: PCOS commonly involves a combination of Kidney deficiency, Phlegm-Damp accumulation, and Liver Qi stagnation. Treatment centers on resolving phlegm, moving and regulating Qi, and reinforcing Kidney function to support overall balance.

conditions.title: Allergies & Hay Fever
conditions.description: Supportive, natural care for seasonal allergies, hay fever, and sensitivities to environmental triggers.
conditions.tcmApproach: Allergic responses frequently signal weakness in Wei Qi — the body's defensive Qi — along with internal imbalances. Our approach builds Wei Qi, clears pathogenic influences, and works to reduce the intensity of inflammatory reactions.

conditions.title: Asthma
conditions.description: Complementary TCM support for asthma, with a focus on reducing both the frequency and severity of episodes.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM associates asthma with deficiency in both the Lung and Kidney, along with phlegm accumulation in the airways. Treatment works to strengthen Lung and Kidney function, resolve phlegm, and reduce overall airway reactivity.

conditions.title: Chronic Cough
conditions.description: Support for a persistent cough that has not resolved with conventional treatment approaches.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM recognizes several distinct patterns that can underlie a chronic cough — including Lung Yin deficiency, Lung Heat, and Phlegm accumulation. Once we identify which pattern applies to you, treatment is tailored to address that specific imbalance at its root.

conditions.title: Frequent Colds & Flu
conditions.description: Strengthening your immune system can help reduce how frequently you fall ill and lessen the severity of colds and respiratory infections when they do arise.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, a tendency to get sick frequently often signals Wei Qi deficiency. We focus on reinforcing Wei Qi, nurturing Lung and Spleen function, and gradually building the body's immune resilience so it is better equipped to stay well over time.
treatmentMethods: Personalized lifestyle guidance and practical recommendations

conditions.title: Chronic Fatigue
conditions.description: Persistent low energy and tiredness that disrupt everyday life can be deeply challenging. Our care is focused on supporting the body's natural vitality and working toward a renewed sense of wellbeing.
conditions.tcmApproach: Through a TCM lens, chronic fatigue frequently reflects Qi deficiency in the Spleen, the Kidneys, or both, and may be accompanied by Blood deficiency as well. Treatment aims to address these underlying deficiencies, supporting the body as it gradually rebuilds its energy and vitality.
treatmentMethods: Practical guidance on finding a healthy balance between rest and physical activity

introduction.note: Don't see your condition listed here? Get in touch with us — chances are we may still be able to help.
introduction.text: At Dr. Henry Smith Clinic, we place the whole person at the center of care — going well beyond symptom management alone. Our approach is comprehensive: we look for the underlying causes of illness while also addressing what is causing discomfort right now. The conditions below reflect a portion of what we regularly support through our practice.
